meaning: A piece of paper or digital document showing that you have paid for something.
example: Keep your receipt in case you need to return the shoes later.

meaning: A bill sent to a customer asking for payment for goods or services provided.
example: The construction company sent an invoice for the materials used.

meaning: A basket on wheels used for carrying goods while shopping in a store.
example: She filled her cart with fresh vegetables and fruits.

meaning: The place in a shop where you pay for your goods.
example: There was a long line at the checkout because only one register was open.

meaning: A passage between rows of shelves in a supermarket or store.
example: You can find the cereal in aisle four, next to the baking supplies.

meaning: A person or business that sells goods directly to the public in small quantities.
example: Large retailers often offer lower prices than small independent shops.

meaning: A person or company that sells goods in large quantities, usually to retailers.
example: The store buys its stock from a wholesaler at a lower price.

meaning: A person who purchases goods and services for personal use.
example: Consumers are demanding more eco-friendly products these days.

meaning: The assistance and advice provided by a company to those who buy or use its products.
example: If your new laptop does not work, call customer service for help.

meaning: Money given by a store to a customer instead of a cash refund, which can only be spent at that store.
example: Since I didn't have the receipt, they gave me store credit instead of cash.

meaning: An amount of money that is given back to you, especially because you are not satisfied with a product.
example: I returned the broken toaster and asked for a full refund.

meaning: To return a product to a store and get a different one instead.
example: The shirt was too small, so I went to the store to exchange it for a medium.

meaning: A written guarantee promising to repair or replace a product if it breaks within a specific period.
example: The television comes with a two-year warranty that covers all repairs.

meaning: Having a fault or flaw; not working properly.
example: The company recalled the defective toys due to safety concerns.

meaning: Available to buy immediately in the store or online.
example: The bookstore had only one copy of the novel in stock.

meaning: Temporarily unavailable because all items have been sold.
example: We are out of stock of that particular phone model, but we can order it for you.

meaning: An order for a product that cannot be delivered now because it is out of stock.
example: My book is on backorder, so it will take two weeks to arrive.

meaning: Looking at goods displayed in shop windows without intending to buy anything.
example: We had no money to spend, so we just spent the afternoon window shopping.

meaning: Buying things suddenly without planning or thinking about whether you really need them.
example: Placing candy near the cash register encourages impulse buying.

meaning: A short period of time that you spend buying a lot of things.
example: After winning the lottery, she went on a major shopping spree.

meaning: A small room in a clothing store where you can try on clothes to see if they fit.
example: She took three dresses into the fitting room to try them on.

meaning: A label attached to an item showing how much it costs.
example: I liked the jacket, but when I looked at the price tag, I realized it was too expensive.

meaning: Purchasing large quantities of a product at one time, usually to save money.
example: Bulk buying toilet paper and paper towels is much cheaper in the long run.

meaning: A well-known name given to a product by the company that makes it.
example: Brand name medicines are usually more expensive than generic ones.

meaning: A large shop divided into several sections, each selling a different type of goods.
example: You can buy clothes, cosmetics, and kitchenware all in one department store.

meaning: A small shop that sells fashionable clothes, shoes, or jewelry.
example: She bought a unique handmade dress from a local boutique.

meaning: A detailed list of all the goods or materials a business has in stock.
example: The store closed early on Sunday to perform their annual inventory count.

meaning: Commercial transactions conducted electronically over the internet.
example: The rise of e-commerce has made it easy to buy products from anywhere in the world.

meaning: A book or online directory showing a list of items for sale with their descriptions and prices.
example: I flipped through the holiday catalog to find gift ideas for my family.

meaning: A small shop that is open for long hours and sells everyday items like snacks, drinks, and newspapers.
example: He ran down to the corner convenience store to buy a carton of milk.

meaning: A system of paying a deposit to secure an item for later purchase, paying the balance in installments.
example: She put the winter coat on layaway and paid ten dollars every week until it was paid off.

meaning: A promise given to a customer allowing them to buy an out-of-stock sale item at the sale price later.
example: The store was out of the discounted shampoo, so they gave me a rain check.

meaning: A person or company offering something for sale, especially on the street or at a market.
example: The street food vendor sells delicious tacos for only two dollars.

meaning: A person or company involved in wholesale trade or retail business.
example: Online merchants must ensure their websites are secure for credit card payments.

meaning: Something bought or offered for sale much cheaper than its usual price.
example: At only ten dollars, this winter coat was a real bargain.

meaning: A reduction in the usual price of something.
example: Students receive a ten percent discount on all public transport tickets.

meaning: A sale in which a shop sells off its old stock at low prices to make room for new items.
example: I bought this pair of boots for half price at the clearance sale.

meaning: A piece of paper or a digital code that gives you a discount when buying a product.
example: She clipped coupons from the newspaper to save money on groceries.

meaning: A document or code that can be exchanged for goods or services of a certain value.
example: The airline gave us a travel voucher as compensation for the delayed flight.

meaning: A short string of letters or numbers used online to get a discount on a purchase.
example: Enter the promo code 'SAVE20' at checkout to get twenty percent off your order.

meaning: Arguing or negotiating about the price of something before reaching an agreement.
example: In local markets, haggling is expected and can save you a lot of money.

meaning: Formal discussion between people who are trying to reach an agreement, such as on a price.
example: After some negotiation, the car dealer agreed to lower the price by five hundred dollars.

meaning: The highest amount of money a buyer is willing to pay, or the lowest a seller is willing to accept.
example: The seller accepted my best offer of three hundred dollars for the antique desk.

meaning: Something that is much more expensive than it should be; a cheat.
example: Charging twenty dollars for a bottle of water at the concert is a total rip-off.

meaning: Costing more than the actual value or more than is reasonable.
example: The restaurant was nice, but the food was overpriced and not very tasty.

meaning: Costing less than the actual value or worth.
example: The house was underpriced, so it sold within two days of listing.

meaning: A promotion where you get a second identical item for free if you buy one.
example: The supermarket has a buy one get one free offer on all fresh berries.

meaning: Costing only fifty percent of the original price.
example: During the summer sale, all shirts were sold at half price.

meaning: A temporary price reduction or bonus deal to encourage people to buy.
example: Take advantage of our special offer: sign up today and get the first month free.

meaning: A scheme where a store rewards regular customers with discounts or free items.
example: I used my points from the store's loyalty program to get a free coffee.

meaning: A reduction in the price of goods to help sell them quickly.
example: The store applied a major markdown to winter clothes in early spring.

meaning: An amount of money returned to a customer after they have paid the full price for a product.
example: There is a fifty-dollar mail-in rebate available for this washing machine.

meaning: Something that is worth the amount of money you pay for it.
example: This cheap smartphone works incredibly well and offers great value for money.

meaning: A bargain that is so good it feels like you are taking it for free.
example: At only five dollars for this brand new book, it was a complete steal.

meaning: A discount of a fixed percentage or amount that applies to all items without exceptions.
example: The store is offering a flat thirty percent discount on everything today.

meaning: A policy where a store agrees to sell an item for the same low price offered by a competitor.
example: They offer a price match guarantee, so I showed them the cheaper price online.

meaning: A sale held at the end of a season to sell off seasonal goods.
example: I always buy my winter coats during the spring seasonal sale when prices drop.

meaning: Sold in its current condition, including any faults, without a warranty or possibility of return.
example: The damaged dining table was sold as-is for a very low price.

meaning: The standard, advertised price of an item before any discounts are applied.
example: We managed to buy the car for two thousand dollars below the list price.

meaning: A bank account where you deposit money to save it, which usually earns interest over time.
example: It is smart to transfer a portion of your monthly salary to a savings account.

meaning: A bank account used for everyday transactions, from which money can be easily withdrawn or spent.
example: I use my checking account to pay my monthly rent and buy groceries.

meaning: The total amount of money in a bank account at a specific time.
example: Please check your account balance to make sure you have enough money for the transfer.

meaning: The percentage of a sum of money charged by a lender or paid by a bank to a saver.
example: The bank raised its interest rate on savings accounts to four percent.

meaning: To put money into a bank account.
example: I need to go to the bank to deposit this check into my account.

meaning: The act of taking money out of a bank account.
example: She made a cash withdrawal of one hundred dollars from the ATM.

meaning: A payment method where money is sent electronically straight into a recipient's bank account.
example: My salary is paid via direct deposit, so it is in my account every Friday morning.

meaning: A printed or digital record showing all the deposits, withdrawals, and fees in a bank account.
example: I review my monthly bank statement to track my spending habits.

meaning: An arrangement or situation where you withdraw more money from your account than you actually have.
example: Her account went into overdraft after the rent check was processed.

meaning: A charge imposed by a bank when a customer withdraws more money than is available in their account.
example: I had to pay a thirty-dollar overdraft fee because my balance fell below zero.

meaning: A bank account shared by two or more people, typically family members or business partners.
example: My husband and I opened a joint account to manage our household expenses together.

meaning: A local office of a bank or other large organization.
example: I need to visit the local branch to speak with a loan officer.

meaning: A bank employee who deals directly with customers, handling deposits and withdrawals.
example: The bank teller greeted me warmly and helped me deposit my money.

meaning: A machine that dispenses cash and performs other banking services when you insert a bank card.
example: I need to find an ATM to withdraw some cash before going to the market.

meaning: A secret number that you use with a bank card to withdraw money or make payments.
example: Never share your PIN with anyone, not even bank employees.

meaning: A locked box inside a bank vault used for storing valuable items like jewelry or important papers.
example: We keep our property deeds and old family gold in a safe deposit box.

meaning: A nine-digit code that identifies a specific financial institution in banking transactions.
example: You will need both your bank routing number and account number to set up direct deposit.

meaning: A unique set of numbers used to identify a specific bank account.
example: Please double-check your account number before confirming the transfer.

meaning: A service that allows customers to conduct financial transactions using a smartphone app.
example: Mobile banking makes it easy to deposit checks using your phone camera.

meaning: Conducting banking transactions over the internet through a bank's website.
example: I logged into online banking to pay my utility bills last night.

meaning: A savings product that earns a fixed interest rate in exchange for keeping your money untouched for a set period.
example: He put his savings into a one-year certificate of deposit to get a higher interest rate.

meaning: The lowest amount of money that a customer must keep in a bank account to avoid fees.
example: This checking account requires a minimum balance of five hundred dollars.

meaning: A situation where many customers rush to withdraw their money from a bank because they fear it will fail.
example: The government stepped in to prevent a bank run after rumors spread about the bank's stability.

meaning: A professional who helps individuals manage their money, investments, and financial planning.
example: We met with a financial advisor to plan for our children's college education.

meaning: A number representing a person's creditworthiness, based on their history of paying back debts.
example: Paying your credit card bills on time is the best way to improve your credit score.

meaning: A cooperative financial institution owned and controlled by its members, often offering lower fees.
example: She decided to join a local credit union instead of a major national bank.

meaning: A person designated to receive the funds or assets from an account in the event of the owner's death.
example: He listed his daughter as the sole beneficiary of his savings account.

meaning: The ease with which assets or savings can be quickly converted into cash without losing value.
example: Cash in a checking account has high liquidity, while real estate has low liquidity.

meaning: Interest calculated on both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods.
example: Thanks to compound interest, even small savings can grow significantly over thirty years.

meaning: An asset that a borrower offers to a lender as security for a loan.
example: She had to put up her house as collateral to secure the business loan.

meaning: To fail to pay a debt or loan by the required time.
example: If you default on your car loan, the bank may take the car back.

meaning: The original sum of money lent or invested, not including the interest.
example: Her monthly mortgage payments go toward paying off both the principal and the interest.

meaning: The process of gradually paying off a debt over time through regular, equal payments.
example: The bank provided an amortization schedule showing how the loan would be paid off over fifteen years.

meaning: The maximum amount of money a credit card company allows you to borrow or charge on your card.
example: He reached his credit limit and could not make any more purchases until he paid off his balance.

meaning: The yearly interest rate charged for borrowing money or earned through an investment.
example: Compare the APR of different credit cards to find the one with the lowest borrowing costs.

meaning: A specific type of loan used to purchase a house or real estate, where the property is security for the loan.
example: They took out a thirty-year mortgage to buy their first home.

meaning: An amount of money that is owed to a person, bank, or company.
example: He worked hard to pay off all his college debt within five years.

meaning: A legal status of a person or organization that cannot repay the debts they owe to creditors.
example: The retail chain declared bankruptcy after several years of falling sales.

meaning: A person who promises to pay a debt if the borrower fails to do so.
example: Since the student had no income, her father signed as a guarantor for the apartment lease.

meaning: An instance of buying or selling something, or moving money between accounts.
example: The online store sends an email confirmation for every transaction you complete.

meaning: Money in the physical form of coins or notes.
example: I prefer to carry a little cash in my wallet in case card machines are down.

meaning: A plastic card that allows you to buy goods now and pay for them later, usually with interest.
example: If you do not pay off your credit card bill in full each month, you will owe interest.

meaning: A plastic card that automatically deducts money directly from your checking account when you buy something.
example: I paid for the groceries using my debit card, and the money was immediately taken from my account.

meaning: A payment method where you pay by tapping your card or phone against a reader without entering a PIN.
example: Contactless payment is very popular because it is fast and convenient.

meaning: An app on a smartphone that stores digital versions of your credit or debit cards for making payments.
example: I left my physical wallet at home, but I was able to pay using the mobile wallet on my phone.

meaning: An electronic transfer of funds from one bank account to another, often used for international payments.
example: They sent the money via wire transfer, and it arrived in my account the next day.

meaning: The electronic transfer of money from one bank account to another, covering all digital payments.
example: Direct deposits and online bill payments are both examples of electronic funds transfer.

meaning: A payment document guaranteed by a bank, typically used for large payments where cash is not practical.
example: The home buyer presented a bank draft to complete the purchase of the house.

meaning: A check guaranteed by the bank itself, signed by a cashier, and paid from the bank's own funds.
example: The landlord required a cashier's check for the security deposit and the first month's rent.

meaning: A printed order for a pre-specified sum of money, purchased at a post office or bank, used as a secure payment.
example: He sent a money order to pay his electricity bill since he did not have a checking account.

meaning: Money sent by a person working abroad back to their family or home country.
example: Remittances from workers overseas play a huge role in the economy of many developing nations.

meaning: An extra fee added to the cost of a good or service, often for using a specific payment method.
example: The gas station charges a small surcharge if you pay with a credit card instead of cash.

meaning: A charge applied by a bank or payment processor for handling a specific transaction.
example: Some international websites charge a transaction fee for processing foreign currencies.

meaning: The period of time between billing statements, usually lasting about thirty days.
example: My credit card billing cycle runs from the first of the month to the last day.

meaning: The date on which a billing statement is generated, summarizing all activity during that billing cycle.
example: Check your statement date to see which purchases are included in this month's bill.

meaning: The final date by which a payment must be received to avoid late fees or interest charges.
example: Make sure to pay your utility bill before the due date next Tuesday.

meaning: The lowest amount of money you are required to pay on a credit card bill to keep the account active.
example: Paying only the minimum payment will result in high interest charges on the remaining balance.

meaning: A transaction that has been authorized but not yet finalized or deducted from your account.
example: The hotel stay is still a pending transaction on my credit card statement.

meaning: An arrangement where a bank is authorized to automatically pay money to a company regularly.
example: I set up a direct debit so my gym membership fee is paid automatically every month.

meaning: A banking feature that automatically pays your recurring bills from your account on the due date.
example: I set my internet bill to auto-pay so I never forget to pay it.

meaning: A notification sent by a bank warning you of suspicious or unauthorized activity on your account.
example: I received a fraud alert text when someone tried to use my card in another country.

meaning: A return of funds to a consumer, initiated by their bank, after they successfully dispute a charge.
example: The customer requested a chargeback because the item they ordered online never arrived.

meaning: An additional charge for handling the administration or technical steps of a transaction.
example: The ticket seller charges a five-dollar processing fee for online bookings.

meaning: A document confirming that payment has been successfully received for a service or product.
example: After you click buy, a receipt of payment will be sent to your email address.

meaning: The address associated with a customer's specific credit or debit card, where statements are sent.
example: Make sure the billing address you enter matches the address on your bank file.

meaning: A charge for using an automated teller machine, especially one that does not belong to your bank.
example: I had to pay a three-dollar ATM fee for withdrawing cash at a different bank's machine.

meaning: A lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts; an error or mismatch.
example: There was a discrepancy between the price on the shelf and the price charged at the register.

meaning: A transaction on a bill or statement that a customer claims is incorrect or fraudulent.
example: She contacted her bank to file a disputed charge for a purchase she did not make.

meaning: A tip given to a waiter, driver, or other service worker for their service.
example: A fifteen percent gratuity was automatically added to our restaurant bill.

meaning: A fee paid to use a specific road, bridge, or tunnel.
example: Make sure you have cash or an electronic pass to pay the toll on the highway.

meaning: The money a passenger on public transportation has to pay.
example: The train fare went up by fifty cents starting this month.

meaning: A periodic report sent to a credit cardholder showing recent transactions and the balance due.
example: I received my monthly billing statement in the mail today.
